aliases: Swiss roll cake with cream filling

english: Swiss roll cake with cream filling

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a Swiss roll entry (FOODON_03315091) which is the closest match; London roll is a Singaporean commercial variant of the Swiss roll with cream filling. Exact FoodOn entry for 'London roll' does not exist.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Swiss roll) used as closest defensible match. London roll is a Singaporean commercial variant; no dedicated FoodEx2 code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for London roll or Swiss roll cake in the clinical terminology system.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of London roll is one individual roll weighing approximately 60 g, as commonly sold in Singaporean bakeries and convenience stores.

nutrient_source.title: London Roll (Any Flavour) — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'London roll (any flavour)', CR ID F11100013. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Sponge cake, plain — Glycemic Index Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for London roll. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain sponge cake (GI ~65), which shares a similar flour-egg-sugar sponge base. The cream filling and sugar content of London roll are comparable to plain sponge cake. GL per serving calculated as: (65 × 54.66 × 0.60) / 100 = 21.4 g per 60 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (24.83 g/100 g) driving insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium at 337 mg/100 g with minimal potassium offset (~90 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit London roll consumption, as the combination of high sugar (24.83 g/100 g) and moderate sodium (337 mg/100 g) with negligible potassium promotes fluid retention and elevates blood pressure; if consumed, restrict to no more than one small slice occasionally.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high sugar (24.83 g/100 g) and high refined carbohydrate (54.66 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (2.5 g/100 g), estimated GI 65 (medium), GL 21.4 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: London roll is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very high sugar content (24.83 g/100 g), high refined carbohydrate load (54.66 g/100 g), and low fibre (2.5 g/100 g), which will cause significant acute postprandial blood glucose elevation; it should be avoided or limited to a very small occasional portion paired with protein or fat to partially attenuate the glycaemic spike.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~8.5 g/100 g), estimated trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g from vegetable shortening), and high sugar (24.83 g/100 g) contributing to atherogenic lipid profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: London roll poses a high cardiovascular risk due to its elevated saturated fat (~8.5 g/100 g), estimated trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g from vegetable shortening), and high sugar content (24.83 g/100 g), all of which promote LDL elevation, endothelial dysfunction, and systemic inflammation; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this product or treat it as a rare indulgence in very small portions.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g), high saturated fat (~8.5 g/100 g), and high refined sugar (24.83 g/100 g) promoting pro-inflammatory cytokine activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: London roll is strongly pro-inflammatory for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to estimated trans fat from vegetable shortening (~0.2 g/100 g), high saturated fat (~8.5 g/100 g), and high refined sugar (24.83 g/100 g); it should be avoided as even small amounts of trans fat are associated with increased inflammatory markers in RA.

llm_training.natural_language_description: London roll is a popular Singaporean commercial bakery snack consisting of a soft, lightly sweetened sponge cake rolled around a cream filling, available in multiple flavours such as pandan, chocolate, strawberry, and vanilla. It is structurally similar to a Swiss roll but is produced at an industrial scale using wheat flour, eggs, sugar, vegetable shortening, and flavoured cream fillings. The texture is soft and moist with a sweet, creamy interior. London rolls are widely sold in Singaporean bakeries, supermarkets, and convenience stores, and are commonly consumed as a snack or light dessert. Nutritionally, London roll is energy-dense at 420 kcal per 100 g, with high refined carbohydrate content (54.66 g/100 g), very high sugar (24.83 g/100 g), and significant fat (19.16 g/100 g) including estimated saturated fat of approximately 8.5 g/100 g and trans fat of approximately 0.2 g/100 g from vegetable shortening. Dietary fibre is low at 2.5 g/100 g. Sodium is 337 mg/100 g. These nutritional characteristics make London roll unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. The estimated glycemic index is 65 (medium), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 21.4 per standard 60 g serving, which represents a significant acute glycaemic challenge. Calcium and iron are reported as 0 mg/100 g in the HPB source. Consumers should treat London roll as an occasional indulgence rather than a regular dietary component.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a London roll?
Is London roll suitable for hypertension?
What is the sugar content of London roll?
Is London roll the same as Swiss roll?

ingredient_synonyms.cream filling: whipped cream or non-dairy cream filling

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F11100013) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and trans fat are estimated from typical London roll formulation using vegetable shortening; these are documented as estimates. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (plain sponge cake) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Multilingual names are provided for all mandatory Singaporean cuisine languages.
notes: Calcium_mg is reported as 0.0 and iron_mg is reported as 0.0 per the HPB Singapore Foodo source (CR ID F11100013); these are confirmed zero values per the approved source and are not naturally zero for all bakery products — the HPB record explicitly reports 0 for these minerals, likely reflecting the specific commercial formulation tested. Saturated_fat_g (~8.5 g/100 g) and trans_fat_g (~0.2 g/100 g) are estimated from typical London roll formulation using vegetable shortening; these are not directly reported in the HPB source. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid breakdown data for London roll are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. Potassium_mg (~90 mg/100 g) is estimated from analogous sponge cake data in USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy). GI of 65 is an analogous estimate from plain sponge cake per the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for London roll exists in the approved GI source. Halal status is not confirmed for all commercial variants; consumers should verify product labelling.
